summary: loader1 fetches and executes remote HTML/JS from a mutable Bitbucket branch with anti-analysis gating
details: The package's declared main, index.html, fetches https://bitbucket.org/p2p-alt-public/p2p-emis/raw/main/GameWebSight (mutable `main` branch, no version pin, no integrity check) and renders the response into the live document via `document.open(); document.write(html); document.close();`. The fetch is gated by a primitive interaction-score check (mouse/scroll/key/click events must accumulate score>3) and a fallback 8-second kill timer that replaces the page with 'Access blocked' if no interaction is detected — a sandbox/headless-analyzer evasion pattern designed to hide the payload from automated scanners while delivering it to real users. The remote source is an attacker-controlled, mutable third-party repository unrelated to any declared publisher; whoever controls that branch can change the executed content at any time. Any site, build, or consumer shipping loader1 will execute whatever HTML/JS is currently hosted at that URL in their users' browsers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
